arcs.title: The Duck Bandit
arcs.summary: The Heron Shelf duck has yielded a note: 'Nice find. — B.' Small, neat hand, no registry mark. A retired tracker confirmed the Bandit knew about Christine's second overhang — meaning they arrived after Christine and Steve did, or were watching. The eastern approach tracks are now a lead. Christine has asked the right question. Day 3 should bring a witness, a vehicle description, or a second duck somewhere unexpected — the Bandit appears to be escalating.

arcs.title: The Glowing Briefcase
arcs.summary: The briefcase is now gold, humming, and resident on Level Two. It has begun emitting a sound that everyone recognises but nobody can place — described as 'the sound of knowing where you left something important.' The Artefact Office has noted what may be a monogram on the lid, unrecognised in any registry. The Ministry continues to explain nothing. Day 4 should bring a partial identification — someone who has seen that monogram before, or a record that almost matches — moving toward a Day 5 reveal of origin or ownership.
